goal: wave4-auth: Wave 4 — Google OAuth and assessment save/resume. Deliver L3 Google OAuth (GET /api/auth/google, callback, logout, GET /api/auth/me) with users + sessions tables; per-host HttpOnly session cookie on first-party /api via combined proxy — same Google account = same user row across .io and .org (separate cookies per TLD, one-click re-auth on second domain). L4 GET/POST /api/assessment/session for partial answers + step index when authenticated. L8/L9 sign-in/out in site chrome + /join on .org; assessment wizard resume when logged in; attach user_id to inquiries when session present. SQLite for Wave 4; env hook for future DATABASE_URL. Document GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, SESSION_SECRET in docs/AGENT_ENV.md (no secrets committed). Anonymous assessment still works without login. See docs/project-progress.md Wave 4 locked decisions.
summary: Wave 4 auth: Google OAuth + sessions, assessment save/resume, sign-in chrome on .io/.org
acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes GET /api/auth/google, GET /api/auth/callback/google, POST /api/auth/logout, GET /api/auth/me with users + sessions tables; auth routes degrade gracefully (501/disabled) when Google env vars are absent so the app runs in dev without them
Same Google account resolves to the same users row regardless of which site initiated sign-in; session cookie is per-host HttpOnly (no cross-TLD cookie)
GET/POST /api/assessment/session save and resume partial answers + step index for an authenticated user; anonymous assessment still works with nothing saved
POST /api/inquiries attaches user_id when a session is present and still works anonymously
Both sites show sign-in/out in the header; .org has a /join page; the .io assessment wizard resumes saved progress when logged in
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, SESSION_SECRET documented in docs/AGENT_ENV.md (no secret values committed); SQLite remains with an env hook for future DATABASE_URL
All workspace packages typecheck, backend tests pass, both Next apps build, and the wave ships as a single draft PR to main

tasks.name: backend-auth
tasks.scopedGoal:
Implement the Wave 4 backend for the ai-transformation pnpm+turbo monorepo: Google OAuth (L3), session middleware + DB (L2), assessment save/resume (L4), shared types/schemas (L0), and docs. This is the ONLY task touching packages/shared, apps/backend, docs/AGENT_ENV.md, .env.example, and data/simulators/auth. Do NOT touch apps/web-io, apps/web-org, or apps/combined. Read docs/project-progress.md 'Wave 4' locked decisions and apps/backend/src/lanes/auth/INTERFACE.md + apps/backend/src/lanes/assessment/INTERFACE.md before coding.

Build EXACTLY to this contract (the frontend task is built independently against the same contract, so do not deviate):

=== DB (apps/backend/src/db/index.ts) ===
Keep the existing contributions table + insertContribution. Add idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS migrations for:
- users: id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, google_sub TEXT UNIQUE, email TEXT NOT NULL, name TEXT, picture TEXT, created_at TEXT NOT NULL, last_login_at TEXT. Add a UNIQUE index on email as well.
- sessions: id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, user_id TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TEXT NOT NULL, expires_at TEXT NOT NULL. Index on user_id.
- assessment_sessions: user_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, answers TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}', step_index INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, last_score TEXT, updated_at TEXT NOT NULL.
Add typed helpers: upsertUserByGoogle({ googleSub, email, name, picture }) returning the user row (insert or update existing matched by google_sub, falling back to email match; same Google identity must always map to ONE users row); createSession(userId, ttlMs) returning { id, expiresAt }; getSessionWithUser(sessionId) returning { user, session } or null (treat expired sessions as null); deleteSession(sessionId); getAssessmentSession(userId); upsertAssessmentSession({ userId, answers, stepIndex, lastScore }). Extend insertContribution to accept an optional userId (already supported via the existing input.userId field — confirm harvest passes it).
Add an env-driven DB driver hook for future Postgres WITHOUT implementing Postgres now: read process.env.DATABASE_URL; if it is set and is not a sqlite/file URL, throw a clear 'DATABASE_URL driver not yet supported in Wave 4 (SQLite only)' error at startup; otherwise use the existing SQLite path (SQLITE_PATH ?? 'data/app.db'). Add a `// defer:` comment noting the Postgres adapter is the upgrade path. Keep db files gitignored (already in .gitignore — verify *.db patterns cover users/sessions data).

=== Shared (packages/shared/src/index.ts) ===
KEEP all existing exports. Add Zod schemas + inferred types (additive, do not remove anything): authUserSchema = { id, email, name: nullable/optional, picture: nullable/optional, createdAt: string }; authMeResponseSchema = { ok: true, user: authUserSchema.nullable() }; assessmentSessionStateSchema = { answers: z.record(z.string(), z.number().int().min(1).max(5)), stepIndex: z.number().int().min(0), lastScore: assessmentScoreResponseSchema.nullable().optional() }; assessmentSessionResponseSchema = { ok: true, session: (assessmentSessionStateSchema extended with updatedAt: string).nullable() }; assessmentSessionSaveRequestSchema = assessmentSessionStateSchema. Add createApiClient methods (additive, all using credentials: 'include' and relative-friendly base): getAuthMe(): Promise<AuthMeResponse>, logout(): Promise<{ ok: true }>, getAssessmentSession(): Promise<AssessmentSessionResponse>, saveAssessmentSession(state): Promise<{ ok: true; updatedAt: string }>. Verify with pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared typecheck.

=== Session middleware (L2) ===
Add a Hono middleware (e.g. apps/backend/src/middleware/session.ts) that reads the HttpOnly cookie 'atx_session' via hono/cookie getCookie, resolves getSessionWithUser, and sets c.set('user', user|null) and c.set('session', session|null). Register it in apps/backend/src/index.ts BEFORE the routers, after CORS. Keep GET /api/health and the existing CORS block (credentials:true) intact.

=== Auth lane (apps/backend/src/lanes/auth/index.ts) ===
Export a Hono router mounted at /api/auth in index.ts (app.route('/api/auth', authRouter)). No new npm deps — use global fetch, node:crypto, and hono/cookie.
Config gate: read GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, SESSION_SECRET. If any is missing, treat Google sign-in as NOT configured.
Routes:
- GET /google: if not configured → 501 JSON { ok:false, error:'Google sign-in is not configured' }. Else build the Google consent URL (https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth) with response_type=code, scope 'openid email profile', access_type online, prompt select_account, client_id, redirect_uri, and a CSRF `state`. Derive the external origin from X-Forwarded-Proto/X-Forwarded-Host headers (the combined proxy sets xfwd) falling back to the Host header; redirect_uri = `${origin}/api/auth/callback/google`. Sign state with HMAC-SHA256(SESSION_SECRET) and also store it in a short-lived HttpOnly cookie 'atx_oauth_state' (SameSite=Lax). Optionally encode a safe relative `return` path (default '/'). 302 redirect to Google.
- GET /callback/google: if not configured → 501 JSON. Validate `state` against the cookie + HMAC; on mismatch → 400. Exchange `code` at https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token (grant_type=authorization_code, same redirect_uri). Fetch profile from https://openidconnect.googleapis.com/v1/userinfo with the access token (need sub, email, name, picture). upsertUserByGoogle, createSession (TTL e.g. 30 days), set HttpOnly cookie 'atx_session' = session id with Path=/, SameSite=Lax, Secure when the derived proto is https, NO Domain attribute (host-only → naturally per-TLD). Clear the oauth_state cookie. 302 redirect to the validated relative return path (default '/'). On any OAuth error → 302 redirect to `${origin}/?auth_error=oauth` (do not leak details).
- POST /logout: delete the session row for the cookie if present, clear the 'atx_session' cookie, return 200 { ok:true } (idempotent even with no cookie).
- GET /me: return 200 { ok:true, user } where user is the c.get('user') row mapped to authUserSchema shape, or null when unauthenticated. Never 500 on missing session.

=== Assessment session (apps/backend/src/lanes/assessment/index.ts) ===
Add to the existing assessmentRouter (mounted at /api/assessment): GET /session and POST /session. Both require auth via c.get('user'); when no user → 401 { ok:false, error:'Not authenticated' }. GET returns 200 { ok:true, session } using getAssessmentSession (session is null when none saved; parse stored answers/last_score JSON). POST parses body with assessmentSessionSaveRequestSchema; on invalid → 400 { ok:false, error }; on success upsertAssessmentSession and return 200 { ok:true, updatedAt }. Do not break POST /score or GET /questions.

=== Harvest attribution (apps/backend/src/lanes/harvest/index.ts) ===
In POST /inquiries, if c.get('user') is set, pass userId: user.id to insertContribution; otherwise omit. Anonymous submissions must still return 201.

=== Tests (vitest, already configured) ===
Add apps/backend/src/lanes/auth/auth.test.ts (and/or assessment session test) using app.request() against the Hono app or routers with an in-memory/tmp SQLite (set SQLITE_PATH to a tmp file in the test). Cover, WITHOUT real Google: (1) getSessionWithUser + cookie flow: insert a user+session directly, call GET /api/auth/me with the atx_session cookie → 200 user present; without cookie → 200 user:null. (2) upsertUserByGoogle dedup: same google_sub twice → one users row (proves same Google account = same user across sites). (3) assessment session round-trip: with a valid session cookie, POST /api/assessment/session then GET returns the saved answers + stepIndex; unauthenticated GET/POST → 401. (4) inquiry attribution: POST /api/inquiries with a valid session cookie persists a contributions row whose user_id = the user id; anonymous POST still 201 with null user_id. (5) GET /api/auth/google with env unset → 501.

=== Docs & fixtures ===
Update docs/AGENT_ENV.md: add a Wave 4 auth section documenting GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, SESSION_SECRET (purpose, set in Zeabur env only, never committed), the two required Google redirect URIs (https://ai-transformation.io/api/auth/callback/google and https://ai-transformation.org/api/auth/callback/google plus http://localhost:8080/api/auth/callback/google for local combined dev), and note that the app runs without them (auth routes return 501). Add DATABASE_URL as a documented future hook (SQLite default in Wave 4). Add the names to .env.example (empty values only). Create data/simulators/auth/session.json fixture matching the GET /api/auth/me shape for an authenticated user. NEVER commit real secret values.

Ensure `pnpm install` succeeds from repo root, and that `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend typecheck`, and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` all pass. Manually smoke: start backend with SQLITE_PATH=/tmp/wave4-auth.db PORT=3001 (no Google env) and confirm GET /api/auth/me → {ok:true,user:null}, GET /api/auth/google → 501, GET /api/health → ok.
acceptance: users, sessions, assessment_sessions tables created idempotently on startup; upsertUserByGoogle maps one Google identity to exactly one users row; DATABASE_URL env hook present (throws clear error for non-sqlite) with SQLite default

GET /api/auth/me returns {ok:true,user} for a valid session cookie and {ok:true,user:null} otherwise; POST /api/auth/logout clears the session idempotently; GET /api/auth/google and /callback/google return 501 when Google env vars are unset and perform the OAuth redirect/exchange when set; session cookie is HttpOnly host-only (no Domain attribute)

GET/POST /api/assessment/session save and resume answers + stepIndex for an authenticated user and return 401 when unauthenticated; POST /api/assessment/score and GET /api/assessment/questions still work
POST /api/inquiries attaches user_id when a session is present and still returns 201 anonymously
packages/shared adds auth + assessment-session schemas/types and createApiClient methods without removing existing exports

vitest tests cover the cookie/me flow, Google dedup, assessment session round-trip, 401 paths, inquiry attribution, and the 501-when-unconfigured path; shared+backend typecheck and backend test all pass
docs/AGENT_ENV.md documents GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET, SESSION_SECRET, redirect URIs, and DATABASE_URL hook with no secret values committed
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- From repo root: `pnpm install`.

## Automated
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test`

## Manual
- Start backend WITHOUT Google env: `SQLITE_PATH=/tmp/wave4-auth.db PORT=3001 pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend dev`.
- `curl -s localhost:3001/api/auth/me` → {ok:true,user:null}.
- `curl -i localhost:3001/api/auth/google` → HTTP 501.
- `curl -s localhost:3001/api/health` → {ok:true,...}.
- `curl -i localhost:3001/api/assessment/session` → 401 (no session).
- `curl -i -X POST localhost:3001/api/inquiries -H 'content-type: application/json' --data @data/simulators/harvest/inquiry.json` → 201 {ok:true,id} (anonymous still works).

## Gotchas
- No new npm deps; use global fetch + node:crypto + hono/cookie. Keep GET /api/health + CORS intact. Cookie must be HttpOnly with NO Domain attribute (per-host). Never commit secret values.

tasks.name: frontend-auth
tasks.scopedGoal:
Build the Wave 4 sign-in/out chrome and assessment resume UI on both Next.js App Router frontends (React 19, Tailwind v4). This task ONLY touches apps/web-io and apps/web-org. Do NOT modify packages/shared, apps/backend, or apps/combined, and do NOT depend on any NEW @ai-transformation/shared exports — define small local TypeScript types matching the contract below and use plain fetch with credentials:'include'. Read docs/project-progress.md 'Wave 4' locked decisions first.

Finalized API contract (built independently by the backend task — match it exactly, all paths relative so the combined proxy routes /api to the backend):
- GET /api/auth/me → 200 { ok: true, user: { id: string, email: string, name?: string|null, picture?: string|null, createdAt: string } | null }. user:null means signed out.
- Start sign-in: navigate the browser to `/api/auth/google` (a normal link / window.location, NOT fetch) so Google's redirect works. When auth is unconfigured the backend returns 501; that is acceptable in dev.
- Sign out: POST /api/auth/logout with credentials:'include' → { ok:true }, then refresh auth state (set user to null) and/or reload.
- GET /api/assessment/session → 200 { ok:true, session: { answers: Record<string,number>, stepIndex: number, lastScore?: object|null, updatedAt: string } | null }; 401 when signed out.
- POST /api/assessment/session with credentials:'include', body { answers: Record<string,number>, stepIndex: number, lastScore?: object|null } → { ok:true, updatedAt }; 401 when signed out.
All requests must send credentials:'include'. Resolve the base as `${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL ?? ''}` + path (production uses the relative path via the combined proxy; local dev may override the base).

1) Sign-in/out chrome on BOTH sites. Create a Client Component (e.g. apps/web-io/components/auth-nav.tsx and apps/web-org/components/auth-nav.tsx, each starting with 'use client') that on mount fetches GET /api/auth/me and renders either a 'Sign in' link to /api/auth/google or the signed-in user's name/email with a 'Sign out' button (POST /api/auth/logout). Handle the loading state quietly (render nothing or a subtle placeholder) and treat fetch failure / 501 as signed-out. Embed this component in the existing SiteHeader in apps/web-io/components/site-chrome.tsx and apps/web-org/components/site-chrome.tsx next to ThemeToggle WITHOUT breaking the current server-component layout (site-chrome stays a server component that renders the client auth-nav). Match each site's existing styling/tokens (var(--muted), var(--foreground), var(--accent), font-light, text-sm) — study site-chrome.tsx, theme-toggle.tsx, app/globals.css. Keep .io tone executive/restrained and .org tone warmer/community.

2) .org /join page: create apps/web-org/app/join/page.tsx (server component exporting metadata) rendering a welcoming Harvest Hub join/sign-in screen (heading like 'Join the community') with a prominent 'Continue with Google' link to /api/auth/google and a short explanation of why to join (post stories, save reflections). Match .org editorial styling (study apps/web-org/components/content-page-layout.tsx, page-intro.tsx, existing app/start/page.tsx). Add a 'Join' or sign-in entry point in the .org nav or hero as appropriate (you may add a nav link in site-chrome).

3) Assessment wizard resume on .io (apps/web-io/components/assessment/assessment-wizard.tsx): when the user is signed in (check GET /api/auth/me on mount), enable save/resume. On entering the questions phase, GET /api/assessment/session; if a saved session exists, prefill answers + stepIndex and let the user continue where they left off (show a subtle 'Resumed your saved progress' note and update the intro copy so it no longer claims 'nothing is saved' for signed-in users — keep the anonymous copy for signed-out users). As the user answers / navigates, persist progress via POST /api/assessment/session (debounce or save on step change + on answer change; avoid spamming — saving on Next/Back and on submit is sufficient). Show a 'Progress saved' affordance when signed in. Anonymous users (user:null or 401) must keep the EXACT current behavior: no saving, no errors, the wizard works fully without login. Do not block the wizard if session GET/POST fails — degrade silently to anonymous behavior.

Ensure `pnpm install` succeeds and that `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io build`, and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build` all pass. packages/shared and apps/backend must be untouched.
acceptance: Both .io and .org headers show a Sign in link (to /api/auth/google) when signed out and the user's name/email + Sign out (POST /api/auth/logout) when signed in, via a client component that reads GET /api/auth/me with credentials included
.org has a /join page with a Continue with Google entry point in the Harvest Hub tone
The .io assessment wizard resumes saved answers + stepIndex for signed-in users (GET /api/assessment/session) and persists progress (POST /api/assessment/session) with a Progress saved affordance
Anonymous users keep the existing wizard behavior with nothing saved and no errors even if session endpoints 401 or fail
No new @ai-transformation/shared exports are relied upon; packages/shared and apps/backend are untouched
typecheck and build pass for both @ai-transformation/web-io and @ai-transformation/web-org
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- From repo root: `pnpm install`.

## Automated
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io typecheck` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org typecheck`.
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io build` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build`.

## Manual
- Run a web dev server; with the backend unavailable, confirm the header renders 'Sign in' (treats fetch failure as signed-out) and the assessment wizard still works fully anonymously (no console errors blocking use). Confirm /join renders on .org.

## Gotchas
- auth-nav and the wizard must be Client Components ('use client'); page.tsx files stay server components for metadata. Use window.location/anchor for /api/auth/google (not fetch). All API calls use credentials:'include'. Do not import new shared exports.

tasks.name: merge-wave4-auth
tasks.scopedGoal:
Integration + ship task for Wave 4 (Google OAuth + assessment save/resume). Your clone starts from the backend-auth slice branch. Merge the frontend-auth slice branch into the current branch. The two slices touch disjoint paths (packages/shared + apps/backend + docs vs apps/web-io + apps/web-org), so conflicts are unlikely; if any arise, keep BOTH sides' changes.

After merging, from repo root run `pnpm install`, then verify the whole wave end to end:
- Typecheck all packages: `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/shared typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org typecheck`.
- Backend tests: `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test`.
- Build both web apps: `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-io build` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build`.
- Start the backend WITHOUT Google env (SQLITE_PATH=/tmp/wave4-merge.db PORT=3001): confirm GET /api/auth/me → {ok:true,user:null}; GET /api/auth/google → 501; GET /api/assessment/session → 401; POST data/simulators/harvest/inquiry.json to /api/inquiries → 201 (anonymous still works); GET /api/health → ok.

Then open a draft pull request to main (base main) summarizing Wave 4: Google OAuth (auth routes + users/sessions tables, per-host HttpOnly cookie, graceful 501 without creds), assessment save/resume (/api/assessment/session), sign-in/out chrome on .io and .org + /join on .org, user_id attribution on inquiries, and the docs/AGENT_ENV.md secret documentation. Note in the PR body that full interactive Google OAuth requires real GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET/SESSION_SECRET set in Zeabur and is verified there, not in CI. Do not merge or close the PR. Keep .orchestrate/ out of the PR diff.
acceptance: The frontend-auth slice is merged with the backend-auth slice so the branch contains both backend + frontend changes
All four packages typecheck, backend tests pass, and both web apps build
A draft PR to main is opened summarizing the Wave 4 auth + assessment save/resume work
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- Repo contains both slices after the merge. `pnpm install` from repo root.

## Automated
- Typecheck shared, backend, web-io, web-org; `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test`; build web-io and web-org.

## Manual
- Start backend (no Google env) SQLITE_PATH=/tmp/wave4-merge.db PORT=3001 and run the curl checks for /api/auth/me, /api/auth/google (501), /api/assessment/session (401), /api/inquiries (201 anonymous), /api/health.

## Gotchas
- Disjoint paths; prefer keeping both sides on any unexpected conflict. Keep .orchestrate/ out of the PR diff.

tasks.name: verify-wave4-auth
tasks.scopedGoal: Independently verify the integrated Wave 4 auth + assessment save/resume on the merged branch. Actually run the checks — do not pattern-match. From repo root: `pnpm install`; typecheck shared, backend, web-io, web-org; run `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` and report pass/fail counts; build web-io and web-org. Then start the backend WITHOUT Google env (SQLITE_PATH=/tmp/wave4-verify.db PORT=3001) and confirm: GET /api/auth/me → {ok:true,user:null}; GET /api/auth/google → HTTP 501; GET /api/assessment/session → 401; POST data/simulators/harvest/inquiry.json to /api/inquiries → 201 (anonymous still works); GET /api/health → ok. Inspect the backend tests to confirm they actually exercise (without real Google): the session-cookie→/api/auth/me flow, same-Google-account→one-users-row dedup, the assessment session save/resume round-trip, 401 unauthenticated paths, and inquiry user_id attribution. Confirm the frontend renders sign-in/out chrome on both sites, a /join page on .org, and assessment wizard resume wiring for signed-in users while preserving anonymous behavior. Confirm docs/AGENT_ENV.md documents GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET, SESSION_SECRET, and the DATABASE_URL hook with NO secret values committed. Report a Verification verdict with concrete execution evidence for every merge-wave4-auth acceptance criterion.
acceptance: Verification section includes concrete execution evidence (commands run + outcomes, including backend test pass counts and curl results) for every merge-wave4-auth acceptance criterion
